title: eriogonum alexanderae
intro: Eriogonum alexanderae, also known as Alexandera's buckwheat, is a perennial herb native to North America, belonging to the Polygonaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Eriogonum alexanderae include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and promote the healing of various infections. Eriogonum alexanderae can be prepared as a tea, salve, or tincture, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health concerns.
